title: Release the morning rush, allow peace as the default
subtitle: A release-and-allow practice for clearing the fight-or-flight start to the day.
excerpt: A short energy statement from Day 21 of the SLEEP program. The release names the habit of waking into urgency; the allow opens to a morning that starts from calm rather than catch-up.
sourceNotes: Adapted from Day 21 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive (Rebecca J Page, 2025). Release/allow structure from The Money Zone (Rebecca Page, 2024).

practice.whenToUse: Use first thing in the morning, before looking at your phone.

content.text: A short energy statement from Day 21 of the SLEEP program. For when the morning starts in urgency before any real demand has been made — a bodily habit of racing that pre-dates the to-do list.
I am ready to release the habit of waking into urgency and rush before the day has made a single demand. I release it now. I release it now. I release it now.
I am ready to align with and allow calm, grounded mornings as my new default. I allow it now. I allow it now. I allow it now.
Say each three times, slow, out loud or in your mind.
Drawn from Day 21 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive (Rebecca J Page, 2025). Release/allow structure from The Money Zone (Rebecca Page, 2024).
